atoms.content: Supervised learning trains on labeled data (where the answer is provided). Unsupervised learning trains on unlabeled data to find hidden structures or groupings naturally.
Supervised is learning with a teacher grading your test. Unsupervised is being dropped in a foreign country and figuring out the language by observing.

1. Supervised: Has labels (target variable).
2. Unsupervised: No labels, finds clusters.
3. Supervised is much more common.
Supervised examples: spam detection, house price prediction, image classification. Unsupervised examples: customer segmentation, anomaly detection, topic modeling.
Supervised and Unsupervised are not the only paradigms. Reinforcement Learning (learning from rewards) and Semi-Supervised Learning (mix of labeled and unlabeled data) also exist.
If you have customer purchase data with NO labels and want to group similar customers, is this Supervised or Unsupervised? (Answer: Unsupervised — specifically clustering).
